---
title: "About"
---

I'm naturally curious and learn by doing. Data science fits because it sits at the intersection of the qualitative and the quantitative, and only lands when it's tied to the people acting on it. My background reflects that: psychology at UCSB, a math post-bacc at CUNY, the rest picked up on the job.

## Starting in Support

My first exposure to business data was support at ZocDoc. After enough conversations the same issues cluster in ways that aren't always visible upstream. I started pulling ticket data into spreadsheets and sharing it with product — the habit stuck: start with what's actually happening, then figure out what to do.

## Product Specialist at Square

I joined Square as a product specialist during the swipe-to-chip-to-contactless transition, bridging support and product. Over five years I grew into an analytics role. I built a classifier that automated a big chunk of support case tagging and freed up real CS capacity. Mostly, though, Square is where I learned how much of the value sits at the seam between functions.

In 2017 I also taught a data analytics course at General Assembly — 10 weeks of SQL, stats, and experimentation. Teaching is the fastest way to find out whether you actually understand something.

## Building DS Chops at Atlassian

I joined Atlassian in 2020 as the first data scientist hire for the events team — tight enough scope to build the pipeline end-to-end instead of inheriting one. From there I moved into marketing DS: ICP modeling, buying group analysis, propensity scoring for the enterprise GTM org. Same seam-between-functions work as Square, now with modeling tools behind it.

Toward the end I spent a lot of time on how LLMs change the shape of DS work. They're a real unlock when paired with verification — checking the join, sanity-checking the numbers, pressure-testing the framing before it reaches a stakeholder. I've seen the tooling work well, and I've seen it produce work that looks right and isn't. A lot of my last year was helping the data org find that balance — MCP connections, knowledge files, walking people through their first workflows. That pulled on the same thing I liked about teaching.

## Where I Am Now

Laid off from Atlassian in March 2026 as part of a broader restructure. Based in the SF Bay Area, looking for senior DS roles where the work is close to the business and I can help a team spot the high-leverage AI use cases, build them, and ship them.

## Education

- **B.A. Psychology** — UC Santa Barbara
- **Post-Baccalaureate, Mathematics** — City University of New York
